_comment: ApiGate config — drop into the root of any repo as .apigate.config.json. All fields optional; defaults shown below.
_frameworks: Toggle individual parsers. Useful for muting Fastify on Express-only repos to keep noise out of the report.
_auth: Identifier names that mean 'this endpoint is guarded' when present in the middleware/decorator chain. Add your project's custom auth helpers here. ApiGate cannot inspect what these identifiers DO at runtime — only their declared presence.
_failOn: Exit-code policy. openWriteMethods=true means an unauthenticated POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE causes exit 1 (recommended). openReadMethods=true also fails on unauthenticated GET (strict). drift=true also fails on any spec-drift item. intentionalPublic=true makes the exit-1 gate ignore the public-auth heuristic — i.e. EVERY open endpoint fails (very strict; for compliance audits).
_publicAuthPatterns: Override the built-in public-auth heuristic. null (default) uses DEFAULT_PUBLIC_AUTH_PATTERNS from lib/heuristics.mjs — common login/signup/refresh/health paths. Provide an array of { method, path } pairs to replace entirely, or an empty array [] to disable the heuristic. The exact-match list is deterministic: paths are normalized (`/users/:id` ↔ `/users/{id}` ↔ `/users/<id>`) before comparison.
_excludePaths: Glob list of files whose endpoints are excluded from the report. Applied AFTER parsing — does not speed up scanning.
_severityOverrides: Reserved for future use — currently ApiGate only emits a single posture classification per endpoint.
